A3_gmm_log_counts_3c_bg.source: 3-comp GMM on log(counts) with 0-count cells as log(0.5)

GMM2_log_counts_gte10.source: 2-comp GMM on log(counts) after filtering counts < 10

PeakGauss3_counts_p0.1.source: GMM-3 on log(counts>=1), rightmost component, 0.1st percentile lower tail

PeakGauss3_counts_p1.source: GMM-3 on log(counts>=1), rightmost component, 1st percentile lower tail

FiltGMM3_counts_p0.1.source: GMM-3 on log(counts>=10), rightmost component, 0.1st percentile

FiltGMM4_counts_p0.1.source: GMM-4 on log(counts>=10), rightmost component, 0.1st percentile

FiltGMM5_counts_p0.1.source: GMM-5 on log(counts>=10), rightmost component, 0.1st percentile

MirrorGauss_counts_p0.1.source: Rightmost peak of log-hist + right-side reflection; Gaussian 0.1st pct

GMM3bg_log_density.source: 3-comp GMM on log(density) with 0-density cells at a low floor

GMM3_log_density.source: 3-comp GMM on log(density>0), no zero padding — lower-comp boundary

PeakGauss3_density_p0.1.source: GMM-3 on log(density>0), rightmost component, 0.1st percentile lower tail

PeakGauss3_density_p1.source: GMM-3 on log(density>0), rightmost component, 1st percentile lower tail

MirrorGauss_density_p0.1.source: Rightmost peak of log(density) + right-side reflection; Gaussian 0.1st pct

Joint_Yen_counts_x_Yen_density.source: Yen on counts AND Yen on density (both must pass)

Joint_Otsu_counts_x_Otsu_density.source: Otsu on counts AND Otsu on density (both must pass)

Joint_GMM_counts_x_GMM_density.source: GMM on counts AND GMM on density (both must pass)

Joint_GMM2c10_x_GMM3d.source: GMM2 on log(counts>=10) AND GMM3 on log(density>0) (both must pass)

Joint_PeakGauss3_p0.1.source: Peak-Gauss 0.1st pct on counts AND density (both must pass)

stopping_condition_note: A strategy 'passes' if coreg_coverage >= 0.95 on every subject. Winners are ranked by `max gfp_frac across subjects` (lower is more stringent), which is well-defined for counts-only, density-only, and joint strategies. Ties broken by mean gfp_frac, then by coverage. No fixed-percentile strategy is considered — all methods must be distribution-driven so they generalise to subjects without a coreg table.
